0.q: OE plants had enhanced photosynthetic rates, respiration rates, and transpiration rates compared with the WT or npq1-2 plants after drought or salt treatment.

1.q: Arabidopsis plants overexpressing ChDREB2C were more tolerant to salt stress than WT plants, through increased scavenging capacity of ROS and accumulation of proline.

2.q: heterotopic expression of ChAOX2 increased proline content, and peroxidase and superoxide dismutase activities, while decreasing relative conductivity and the reactive oxygen species level.

3.q: Arabidopsis plants overexpressing ChDREB2C were more tolerant to salt stress than WT plants, through increased scavenging capacity of ROS and accumulation of proline.

4.q: the relative de-epoxidation state of the xanthophyll cycle pigments (A + Z)/(V + A+Z) was higher in OE plants than in the controls

5.q: The interaction between ChABF2 (ABA response element binding factor 2) and ChDREB2C was verified using yeast two-hybrid and firefly luciferase assays.

6.q: The interaction between ChABF2 (ABA response element binding factor 2) and ChDREB2C was verified using yeast two-hybrid and firefly luciferase assays.
